sourceMovie.title: The Pledge
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Pledge works as a crime movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: A Walk Among the Tombstones
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Pledge clicked for you, A Walk Among the Tombstones (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Pledge?
faq.answer: Try A Walk Among the Tombstones, Edge of Darkness, Reptile—each shares DNA with The Pledge while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Pledge?
faq.answer: The Pledge (2001) is best described as Crime, Drama, Mystery, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Pledge worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
